proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_008=1 have higher objective_response rate than those with feature_008=0 (binary main effect, positive direction).
Patients with feature_013=1 have lower objective_response rate than those with feature_013=0 (negative direction).
Patients with feature_015=1 have lower objective_response rate than those with feature_015=0 (negative direction).
Patients with feature_021=1 have lower objective_response rate than those with feature_021=0 (negative direction).
Patients with feature_027=1 have lower objective_response rate than those with feature_027=0 (negative direction).
Higher levels of the ordinal feature_001 (0/1/2) are associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher feature_022 (right-skewed continuous) is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher feature_020 is associated with lower objective_response.
feature_010 (5-level ordinal, 6-10) is not associated with objective_response.
feature_016 (continuous, ~age scale) is not associated with objective_response.
feature_030 has zero variance (single value) and contributes no information.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 0.1589 in feature_008=0 vs 0.3614 in feature_008=1; OR ~2.99; chi-square p < 1e-300.
ORR 0.346 in feature_013=0 vs 0.154 in feature_013=1; OR 0.343; chi-square p < 1e-300.
ORR 0.282, 0.228, 0.182 across feature_001 levels 0, 1, 2; monotone decreasing; chi-square p = 5.4e-72.
Mean feature_022 47.23 in non-responders vs 28.24 in responders; Welch t-test p = 1.5e-137.
Mean feature_020 3.95 in non-responders vs 3.50 in responders; p = 6.8e-31.
ORR essentially flat across feature_010 levels 6-10 (0.244, 0.237, 0.242, 0.242, 0.242); chi-square p = 0.77.
Mean feature_016 64.98 in non-responders vs 65.01 in responders; p = 0.82.
feature_030 has 1 unique value (all zeros) — uninformative; excluded going forward.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strongest binary predictors (feature_008, feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027) are pairwise independent (Pearson correlations near 0).
feature_022 (continuous) is positively correlated with feature_013 (binary), suggesting feature_013 partially marks high-feature_022 disease.

In a multivariable logistic model, the adjusted ORs for feature_008, feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027, feature_001, log(feature_022), log(feature_020), log(feature_024), feature_002 remain similar to unadjusted ORs (no major confounding).

analyses.result_summary: All pairwise Pearson correlations among feature_008/013/015/021/027 are |r| <= 0.008; effective independence.
Pearson r = 0.299 between feature_013 and feature_022 — only meaningful correlation among the strong predictors.

Adjusted ORs: feature_008 OR 3.33 (p<1e-300); feature_013 OR 0.34; feature_015 OR 0.50; feature_021 OR 0.48; feature_027 OR 0.65; feature_001 OR 0.72/level; log(feature_022) OR 0.92; log(feature_020) OR 0.87; log(feature_024) OR 0.91; feature_002 OR 1.14; feature_016 and feature_010 null. Pseudo R^2 = 0.124.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008 is allocated essentially at random with respect to every other measured feature (treatment-like balance), as evidenced by chi-square or t-test p-values being near uniformly distributed and no systematic mean differences.
The effect of feature_008 on objective_response is suppressed by feature_013=1 (strong negative interaction).
The effect of feature_008 on objective_response is suppressed by feature_015=1.
The effect of feature_008 on objective_response is suppressed by feature_021=1.
The effect of feature_008 on objective_response is suppressed by feature_027=1.
The effect of feature_008 on objective_response is attenuated at high feature_022 (apparent interaction may be driven by feature_013 confounding).

Continuous laboratory features (feature_020, 024, 002, 018, 026, 009, 029, 003, 012, 025, 028, 007, 014, 032, 031) and demographic-like feature_016 do not modify the feature_008 treatment effect (interaction p > 0.05 after Bonferroni).

analyses.code: for c in others: chi2/t-test feature_008 vs c
analyses.result_summary: Across 11 other binary features, 2 multilevel features, and 16 continuous features: all chi-square p > 0.04 and all t-test p > 0.06; mean difference of feature_008 = 0.4015 vs 0.4015 essentially. Allocation is balanced — feature_008 behaves as a randomized treatment.
OR(feature_008) = 7.66 when feature_013=0 vs 1.06 when feature_013=1; LRT for interaction p ~ 0 (chi2 ~ 1885).
OR(feature_008) = 3.67 when feature_015=0 vs 1.07 when feature_015=1; interaction p = 7.9e-95.
OR(feature_008) = 3.28 when feature_021=0 vs 1.05 when feature_021=1; interaction p = 1.1e-42.
OR(feature_008) = 3.09 when feature_027=0 vs 0.86 when feature_027=1; interaction p = 2.5e-20.

analyses.code: logit y ~ T + log(f022) + T*log(f022) (full cohort)
analyses.result_summary: Marginal interaction T x log(feature_022): OR(T) = 6.08 at -1 SD log_f022, OR(T) = 1.21 at +1 SD log_f022, interaction p = 1.1e-77 in the full cohort. Sign suggests true interaction OR may be confounded by feature_013.

analyses.code: logit y ~ T + cont_z + T*cont_z for each continuous feature
analyses.result_summary: After Bonferroni across ~16 continuous features (alpha 0.003), no continuous feature shows a treatment-effect interaction with p < 0.003 (smallest: feature_002 p=0.058, feature_025 p=0.035, feature_032 p=0.08). Treatment effect is essentially constant across continuous laboratory values.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a joint multivariable model with feature_008, the four binary modifiers, and four T*modifier terms, each T*modifier coefficient is large and negative (each modifier independently contributes to abolishing the treatment effect).

The subgroup defined by feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0 (the 'all-modifiers-OFF' or favorable subgroup) shows a much higher objective response rate when feature_008=1 than when feature_008=0 (large positive risk difference).
Within the favorable subgroup, the apparent T x feature_022 interaction observed in the full cohort disappears (the univariate interaction was a confound through feature_013).
Among the 16 cells defined by 2^4 modifier patterns, only the all-zero cell shows a meaningful treatment effect; cells with one or more modifiers ON show treatment risk differences near zero.

analyses.result_summary: All four T*modifier coefficients are large and negative: T*feature_013 beta=-2.20 (OR 0.111, p<1e-300); T*feature_015 beta=-1.61 (OR 0.20, p<1e-100); T*feature_021 beta=-1.52 (OR 0.22, p=2e-65); T*feature_027 beta=-1.65 (OR 0.19, p=1e-27). Main feature_008 coefficient becomes 2.58 (OR 13.2 when all modifiers=0).

analyses.code: sub2 = df[fav]; logit y ~ T + log_f022_z + T*log_f022_z
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup, T x log(feature_022) interaction p = 0.34 (LRT chi2 = 0.90). OR(T) at -1 SD = 19.84 vs at +1 SD = 18.33. Effect of treatment is essentially flat across feature_022 once the 4 binary modifiers are accounted for.

16 modifier patterns yield risk differences: 0000 RD=+0.626 (responsive); all 15 other patterns have |RD| <= 0.09 with most around 0.02 and several negative. The treatment effect is exclusively in the 0000 cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup (all 4 modifiers=0), no remaining feature meaningfully modifies the feature_008 treatment effect after multiple-testing correction.
Within the unfavorable subgroup (>=1 modifier=1), no single binary feature creates a sub-subgroup in which feature_008 has a meaningful positive treatment effect (no rescue).
Within the unfavorable subgroup, no PAIR of additional binary features creates a sub-subgroup in which feature_008 has a meaningful positive treatment effect (no 2-feature rescue subgroup).

analyses.code: for each non-modifier feature: LRT for T x feature interaction within favorable subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Across 26 features tested in the favorable subgroup, no T x feature interaction has p < 0.0019 (Bonferroni alpha for 26 tests). Smallest p: feature_005 p=0.004 (OR(T) 22.05 vs 17.24 — both massive). Treatment effect is consistent OR ~19 throughout the favorable subgroup.

analyses.code: for each non-modifier feature in unfavorable subgroup: LRT
analyses.result_summary: Within unfavorable (n=34,319), no feature x T interaction has p < 0.0019 after Bonferroni; smallest is feature_005 p=0.004 (OR(T) 0.95 vs 1.14 — both null). No 'rescue' modifier exists.

analyses.code: exhaustive 2-binary-feature subgroup search within unfavorable; report max RD
analyses.result_summary: Across 1- and 2-feature subgroup conditions of size >=200 within the unfavorable patients, the maximum positive treatment-effect risk difference is +0.090 (feature_006=1 & feature_004=1, n=357, p=0.017 — does not survive multiple comparisons). All others have RD <= 0.05. No clinically meaningful rescue subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 has a strong main negative effect on objective_response (lower ORR at higher levels), independent of treatment.
feature_001 acts as a 5th treatment-effect modifier on the OR scale (T x feature_001 interaction is significant after adjusting for the four binary modifiers).
Within the favorable subgroup, the T effect on the absolute risk-difference scale is somewhat smaller at higher feature_001 levels.

analyses.result_summary: Per-level beta for feature_001 = -0.38 (OR 0.69, p < 1e-50). Main effect strong and monotone.

analyses.code: logit y ~ T + 4 modifiers + 4 T*mod + feature_001 + T*feature_001
analyses.result_summary: After including the four binary modifiers and their T-interactions, the T x feature_001 coefficient is +0.045 (OR 1.05, p = 0.21). feature_001 does NOT act as a 5th modifier on the OR scale; its apparent univariate interaction (p=1.5e-5) was largely confounded by the binary modifiers.
Within the responsive subgroup, RD by feature_001 level: 0 -> +0.6455 (n=5511); 1 -> +0.6219 (n=7824); 2 -> +0.5897 (n=2346). Slight monotone attenuation but huge benefit at every level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining the responsive subgroup using only 3 of the 4 modifiers (dropping any one) leads to a smaller treatment risk difference than using all 4 (because the dropped modifier dilutes the cell with non-responsive patients).
Cells defined by 'exactly k of the 4 modifiers ON' show a treatment risk difference that is +0.626 at k=0 and essentially zero (|RD| < 0.05) at k>=1, regardless of which modifiers are ON.

analyses.code: for each modifier, define subgroup using the other 3 modifiers=0; compute RD
analyses.result_summary: Drop feature_013: RD = +0.287 (n=34,844). Drop feature_015: RD = +0.505 (n=19,621). Drop feature_021: RD = +0.563 (n=17,456). Drop feature_027: RD = +0.607 (n=16,175). All four modifiers must be off for the maximum treatment effect (+0.626).

k=0: RD +0.6257 (p<1e-300, n=15,681). k=1: RD +0.0087 (p=0.066, n=25,372). k=2: RD +0.0075 (p=0.37, n=8,186). k=3: RD -0.0224 (p=0.49, n=745). k=4: RD -0.091 (p=1.00, n=16). The responsive cell is exclusively k=0; the suppression is fully saturated at k>=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An additive log-OR interaction model (T + 4 modifiers + 4 T*modifier) fits significantly worse than a fully saturated model over the 32 cells of T x 4-modifier interactions, indicating the modifiers act in a saturation (non-additive) fashion.

The four modifiers (feature_013, 015, 021, 027) have small or null prognostic effects within untreated patients (T=0), confirming they are predictive (treatment-effect-modifying) rather than prognostic markers.
The four modifiers strongly reduce ORR within treated patients (T=1), confirming their action is to abolish treatment efficacy rather than to mark a generally worse prognosis.

analyses.code: compare LL of saturated cell-mean model vs additive-interaction model
analyses.result_summary: LL_saturated = -22317; LL_additive = -22986; LRT chi2 = 1338, df = 21, p = 1.5e-270. Additive interaction model is grossly inferior — modifier effects on T do NOT follow additive log-OR. The data follow an 'any modifier ON => no T effect' pattern.

analyses.code: within T=0: chi-square ORR by modifier
analyses.result_summary: Among untreated (n=29,924): feature_013 RD on ORR = -0.019 (p=1e-5, small effect); feature_015 RD = -0.003 (p=0.58); feature_021 RD = -0.013 (p=0.07); feature_027 RD = +0.026 (p=0.03, opposite direction). All effects are small-to-null prognostically.

analyses.code: within T=1: chi-square ORR by modifier
analyses.result_summary: Among treated (n=20,076): feature_013 RD = -0.452; feature_015 RD = -0.246; feature_021 RD = -0.231; feature_027 RD = -0.205. All four very large and highly significant (all p << 1e-20). Modifiers act exclusively to suppress treatment efficacy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among all 12 binary features, only feature_008 has BOTH (a) balanced allocation across all other features and (b) a positive ORR effect — confirming feature_008 is the unique treatment-like indicator, and the four modifiers are baseline biomarkers, not alternative treatments.

Pairwise interactions among the 4 modifiers within treated patients are positive on the log-OR scale (interaction OR > 1), reflecting saturation: once one modifier is ON, adding a second doesn't further suppress ORR.
Pairwise interactions among the 4 modifiers within untreated patients are null (modifiers are prognostically additive/independent in untreated patients).

analyses.code: for each binary: prevalence, ORR effect, balance vs other binaries
analyses.result_summary: feature_008 alone: balanced (median balance p=0.45 across other binaries) AND positive direction (OR 2.99). All other binary features either have null effect (feature_006/005/023/011/017/019/004 all OR ~ 1) or are negative-direction modifiers (feature_013/015/021/027). Only feature_008 has the treatment signature.

analyses.code: within T=1: pairwise logit y ~ a + b + a*b
analyses.result_summary: All 6 pairwise interaction ORs among modifiers in T=1 are > 1 (saturation): feature_013xfeature_015 OR=11.5 (p=6e-141); feature_013xfeature_021 OR=11.8 (p=7e-78); feature_013xfeature_027 OR=5.9 (p=1e-13); feature_015xfeature_021 OR=3.6 (p=1e-12); feature_015xfeature_027 OR=3.8 (p=2e-5); feature_021xfeature_027 OR=2.6 (p=0.04). Joint suppression is sub-additive on the log-OR scale.

analyses.code: within T=0: pairwise logit y ~ a + b + a*b
analyses.result_summary: Within untreated patients, 5 of 6 pairwise interactions among modifiers have p > 0.08 (one borderline feature_015xfeature_021 p=0.044). Modifiers act independently/additively on prognosis when no treatment is given.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Bootstrap (2000 replicates) of the responsive subgroup yields a treatment risk difference of approximately +0.626 with a tight 95% CI ([0.61, 0.64]), confirming high precision.
Bootstrap-based 95% CI for the treatment OR within the responsive subgroup is approximately [17.6, 20.6], confirming a robust ~19-fold odds ratio.

analyses.code: B=2000 bootstrap; resample within responsive subgroup; compute RD
analyses.result_summary: Bootstrap mean RD = 0.6257; 95% percentile CI [0.6136, 0.6384]. Highly precise estimate of treatment risk difference in the responsive subgroup.
Bootstrap mean OR = 19.01; 95% percentile CI [17.57, 20.61].

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among UNTREATED patients (T=0), the strongest prognostic predictors of objective response are feature_001 (ordinal), log(feature_022), log(feature_020), log(feature_024), and feature_002.
Among UNTREATED patients (T=0), the four treatment-effect modifiers (feature_013, 015, 021, 027) have negligible adjusted prognostic effects.

analyses.code: logit y ~ all 30 features, restricted to T=0
analyses.result_summary: Within T=0: feature_001 OR 0.68 (p<1e-50); log(feature_022) OR 0.92 (p<1e-50); log(feature_020) OR 0.86 (p<1e-50); log(feature_024) OR 0.91 (p<1e-50); feature_002 OR 1.16 (p<1e-50). These are stable prognostic factors of baseline ORR.

analyses.code: logit y ~ all 30 features, restricted to T=0
analyses.result_summary: Within T=0: feature_013 beta=-0.04 (p=0.27); feature_015 beta=-0.02 (p=0.62); feature_021 beta=-0.10 (p=0.07); feature_027 beta=+0.19 (p=0.03 with positive direction). All small or null prognostically.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among TREATED patients (T=1), the four modifiers are dominant negative predictors of response, with adjusted ORs near 0.10 to 0.23.

Among TREATED patients, prognostic factors that mattered in untreated (feature_001, log(feature_022), log(feature_020), log(feature_024), feature_002) also matter in treated, but with similar magnitude — they are primarily prognostic, not predictive.

analyses.code: logit y ~ all 30 features, restricted to T=1
analyses.result_summary: Within T=1: feature_013 OR 0.104 (p<1e-200); feature_015 OR 0.190 (p<1e-100); feature_021 OR 0.192 (p<1e-50); feature_027 OR 0.228 (p<1e-15). Among treated, modifiers are by far the dominant predictors of failure.

analyses.code: logit y ~ all 30 features, restricted to T=1
analyses.result_summary: Within T=1: feature_001 OR 0.72 (p<1e-30); log(feature_022) OR 0.92; log(feature_020) OR 0.85; log(feature_024) OR 0.91; feature_002 OR 1.13. ORs of these prognostic factors are similar to those in T=0, consistent with prognostic-not-predictive interpretation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Recoding the 4 modifiers into a single 'any_modifier' indicator (1 if any of feature_013/015/021/027 = 1) yields the same treatment-effect heterogeneity: T effect is enormous when any_modifier=0 and null when any_modifier=1.

analyses.result_summary: OR(T) when any_modifier=0: 18.98. OR(T) when any_modifier=1: 1.06. Interaction beta=-2.89, p<1e-300. The collapsed 'any modifier' formulation captures the saturation pattern more parsimoniously than the 4-additive-interactions model and confirms a clean dichotomy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The all-4-modifiers-OFF subgroup definition replicates in two random halves of the cohort: each half independently shows a T-effect risk difference > 0.60 within the responsive subgroup and near zero elsewhere.

analyses.code: split cohort 50/50 by random seed; recompute
analyses.result_summary: Half A (n_responsive=7,867): RD T1-T0 = +0.624; non-responsive RD = +0.013. Half B (n_responsive=7,814): RD = +0.628; non-responsive RD = +0.003. Definition replicates cleanly.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Number-needed-to-treat (NNT) within the responsive subgroup is small (~2), reflecting a large absolute treatment benefit.
Among treated patients, those meeting the all-4-modifiers-OFF rule have ~80% ORR vs ~16% in those who do not, a 5-fold relative difference.

analyses.result_summary: NNT = 1 / 0.6257 = 1.60. Treating 1.6 patients in the responsive subgroup yields one extra responder.

analyses.code: ORR in T=1 by rule status
analyses.result_summary: Among 20,076 treated: 6,325 (31.5%) meet the rule, ORR = 0.7978; 13,751 (68.5%) do not, ORR = 0.1606. Risk difference 0.637.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive subgroup, the absolute treatment risk difference is slightly attenuated as feature_001 increases (i.e., a modest 4th-order modulation of the T effect), but treatment remains highly beneficial at every feature_001 level.

analyses.result_summary: Within responsive: feature_001=0 -> RD +0.646, feature_001=1 -> +0.622, feature_001=2 -> +0.590. Modest decline (~5 pp from level 0 to level 2) but still huge benefit at every level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive subgroup, splitting at the median of any continuous feature does not produce a meaningful (>0.05 absolute) modulation of the treatment risk difference — i.e., no continuous lab-based modifier exists.

analyses.code: median split of each continuous feature within responsive subgroup, compute RD difference
analyses.result_summary: Across 17 continuous features, the maximum absolute difference in RD between halves is 0.022 (feature_014). All other features yield differences <= 0.018. No continuous modifier of treatment effect exists within the responsive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among untreated patients (T=0), the prognostic effects of feature_022, feature_020, feature_024, feature_002 are essentially linear on the log-odds scale (no significant quadratic component).

analyses.code: logit y ~ x + x^2 within T=0 for each continuous feature (z-scored)
analyses.result_summary: feature_022: x p=2e-7, x^2 p=0.28. feature_020: x p<1e-12, x^2 p=0.96. feature_024: x p=3e-5, x^2 p=0.29. feature_002: x p=3e-6, x^2 p=0.38. No quadratic effect; linear log-odds suffices.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive subgroup, the treated-arm ORR is ~80% (not 100%), implying intrinsic (non-modifier-explained) variation in response that we cannot further partition with the available features.

analyses.code: ORR in T=1 within responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Treated ORR within responsive subgroup = 5046/6325 = 0.7978; 20.2% of treated favorable patients still do not respond. Residual non-response is uniform across remaining features (no further modifier identified — see iter 5 and 17).

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL refined hypothesis: feature_008 is a randomized treatment indicator. The treatment-effect-positive subgroup is fully defined by the joint condition feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0 (~31.4% of patients). Within this responsive subgroup, treatment increases objective response from ~17.2% to ~79.8% (RD +62.6 pp; OR ~19; bootstrap 95% CI on RD [0.614, 0.638]). Outside this subgroup, treatment has no effect (RD < +0.01; OR ~1.06). Each of the four modifiers is individually sufficient to abolish the treatment effect (saturation), and their joint action is consistent with an 'any-resistance-marker-positive' rule rather than additive log-OR suppression.

analyses.code: consolidated cell-by-cell ORR table + bootstrap + necessity tests (iter 4, 7, 10, 13, 14)
analyses.result_summary: Across all preceding analyses: responsive subgroup (n=15,681) RD = +0.6257 [0.614, 0.638], OR 19.0 [17.6, 20.6], chi-square p < 1e-300. Non-responsive subgroup (n=34,319) RD = +0.0077 (OR 1.06, p=0.20 in joint model). All sensitivity checks (half-sample validation, dropping one modifier, recoding to any_modifier) reproduce the same conclusion. No further modifiers discovered.
